## Cases mentioned in passing

### Weld failure in overloaded metal part
- **canonical_cluster_id:** "Weld failure in overloaded metal part"
- **Anchor:** `§8.p5`
- **Quote (layer 3):** "Another heuristic I use in my consulting: people call me because, well, the weld failed, the weld broke, the part's defective, it's a defective weld. I look at the part and say, well, did the metal bend and deform before the weld broke or not? Many times I see the whole thing has been stretched. If the metal stretched before the weld broke, the weld did its job. No one designs these things to start deforming before the weld would break. If the weld held until then, but then eventually it was the weld that failed first after the thing had been overloaded terribly, then what's wrong with the..."
